source_name: Code4rena Reserve Protocol Invitational Findings and Analysis Report (2023-07)
finding_title: Possible rounding during the reward calculation
summary: Some rewards might be locked inside the contract due to the rounding loss.
root_cause: reward distribution math loses value through rounding when proportional reward calculations truncate without fair remainder handling
broken_invariants: reward accounting must not systematically strand or misallocate truncated remainders
small holders or early exiters must not lose rewards due to repeated proportional rounding
reward distribution should preserve total distributable value across claims
attack_preconditions: the affected Reserve collateral/plugin/wrapper is deployed or configured in an RToken basket
the report-described oracle, reward, wrapper, or integration edge case is reachable in the audited code version
attacker_capability: external user, trader, or protocol participant able to trigger the affected pricing/trading/reward/withdrawal flow
trigger_actions: execute or wait for the report-described state transition
observe mispricing, reward misallocation, fund lock, or availability failure

impact.description: Some rewards might be locked inside the contract due to the rounding loss.

why_not_higher: final C4 severity is Medium; impact, preconditions, or scope are bounded relative to High
why_not_lower: included as a validated High/Medium C4 finding with concrete affected code paths and impact narrative
common_false_positive_angles: verify the exact collateral plugin/wrapper is in scope and configured
check whether oracle freshness, decimals, or external integration assumptions differ in the current target
confirm the issue affects value movement, redemption availability, or reward ownership rather than a harmless view mismatch
mitigations: I think there would be 2 mitigations. - Use a bigger multiplier. - Keep the remainders and use them next time in _claimAndSyncRewards() like this. if (balanceAfterClaimingRewards > _previousBalance) { uint256 delta = balanceAfterClaimingRewards - _previousBalance; //new rewards uint256 deltaPerShare = (delta * one) / _totalSupply; //new delta per share // decrease balanceAfterClaimingRewards so remainders can be used next time balanceAfterClaimingRewards = _previousBalance + deltaPerShare * _totalSupply / one; _rewardsPerShare += deltaPerShare; } lastRewardBalance = balanceAfterClaimingRewards; ### Assessed type Math

snippets.note: link preserved from Code4rena report or mitigation review
